page: Amazon Web Services hpc6a.48xlarge is an x86_64 architecture server optimized for high-performance computing workloads. It features 96 dedicated physical AMD EPYC 7R13 cores operating at 3.6 GHz, with a single thread per core configuration. The system is equipped with 384.0 GB of DDR4 memory running at 3 GHz, yielding a resource density of 4.0 GB of RAM per core. There is no local NVMe storage or GPU acceleration bundled with this instance type, meaning all storage requirements must be handled via network-attached options. Network connectivity is supported by a baseline bandwidth of 100 Gbps.

Benchmark data indicates top-tier multi-core CPU performance, with stress-ng multi-core and PassMark CPU scores placing in the top 10% of tested systems. Memory performance is also highly rated, with small-block read-only memory bandwidth and PassMark memory scores reaching the top 10%. Multi-threaded bzip3 decompression, software compilation with Clang, and ray tracing benchmarks similarly rank in the top 10%. However, single-threaded performance is more moderate, with Geekbench single-core and single-threaded compression tests scoring in the middle 50%. Large-model LLM inference represents a notable bottleneck, with text generation on a medium 7B model falling into the bottom 25%.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the hpc6a.48xlarge offers high compute density and memory bandwidth for parallelized workloads without the added expense of GPU hardware. The absence of hyperthreading ensures predictable execution times for compute-bound tasks, though it limits total concurrent thread count compared to SMT-enabled instances. The lack of local storage requires reliance on network storage, which may introduce latency tradeoffs depending on the workload.

This server is designed for highly parallel, compute-intensive workloads. Appropriate applications include computational fluid dynamics, weather forecasting, molecular dynamics, software compilation, and large-scale simulations. It is also suitable for high-throughput Redis deployments and parallel data processing tasks that benefit from dedicated physical cores and high network throughput.
description: Amazon Web Services hpc6a.48xlarge is an x86_64 high-performance computing server powered by 96 dedicated physical AMD EPYC 7R13 cores running at 3.6 GHz. It features 384.0 GB of DDR4 memory, providing 4.0 GB of RAM per core, and operates on the Nitro hypervisor. The instance does not include local storage or GPU accelerators, but offers a 100 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. Benchmark results place its multi-core CPU and memory bandwidth performance in the top 10%, making it highly capable for parallel processing, though single-core metrics and medium-to-large LLM text generation speeds are comparatively lower. This server is optimized for compute-heavy parallel workloads such as scientific simulations, software compilation, and high-throughput database operations, offering predictable performance due to its single-thread-per-core design.
og_description: Amazon Web Services hpc6a.48xlarge is an x86_64 server featuring 96 dedicated AMD EPYC cores, 384 GB DDR4 RAM, and 100 Gbps network bandwidth, optimized for parallel high-performance computing workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services hpc6a.48xlarge features 96 dedicated AMD EPYC cores, 384 GB RAM, and 100 Gbps bandwidth for high-performance computing.
tagline: A dedicated ninety-six core AMD EPYC platform with high-speed networking and memory bandwidth for parallel compute-intensive workloads.
bullet_points: 96 dedicated physical AMD EPYC 7R13 cores with no hyperthreading
384 GB of DDR4 memory providing 4.0 GB per core
100 Gbps baseline network bandwidth for high-throughput clustering
Top-tier multi-core CPU and memory bandwidth benchmark performance
No local storage or GPU accelerators included
Suited for scientific simulations, software compilation, and parallel computing
